[[Paul Sampson]] (cousin) }} }} '''Dean Sampson''' (born 27 June 1967) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}}, and spent the majority of his professional career at the [[Castleford Tigers]], with spells in Australia for [[Gold Coast Seagulls|Gold Coast]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]]. Sampson made over 400 appearances for Castleford (Tigers) between 1987 and 2005. He also represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level, and was selected to go on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australia and New Zealand]]. ==Background== Sampson was born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England. ==Playing career== ===Castleford=== Sampson started his career at [[Stanley Rangers]] before joining [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in 1986, and made his first team début in 1987. During the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]], Sampson appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|hk}} [[Kevin Beardmore]]) in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 12–12 draw with [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1987 Yorkshire Cup]] Final at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 17 October 1987, and appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|pr}} [[John Fifita]]) in the 2–11 defeat by Bradford Northern in the [[Replay (sports)|replay]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 31 October 1987. Sampson appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing [[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] [[David Roockley]]) in the 12–33 defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[1988 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1988–89 Rugby Football League season|1988–89 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 16 October 1988, and played {{rlp|pr}} in the 11–8 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[1990 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 23 September 1990. Sampson was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|pr}} [[Lee Crooks (rugby league)|Lee Crooks]] on 33-minutes) in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 12–28 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], London on Saturday 2 May 1992, in front of a crowd of 77,386.{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3066|title=2nd May 1992: Wigan 28 Castleford 12 (Challenge Cup Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|access-date=1 January 2017|archive-date=15 February 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170215030604/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3066|url-status=dead}} Sampson appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|pr}} [[Martin Ketteridge]] on 74-minutes) in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 33–2 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1993–94 League Cup (rugby league)|1993–94 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 22 January 1994.{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|title=Wigan 2 – 33 Castleford|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=27 July 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727175348/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|url-status=dead}} Sampson initially retired from playing at the end of the 2002 season, but came back in 2003 when Castleford Tigers went through an injury crisis,{{cite web |title=Sampson set for shock return |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3050857.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=25 March 2025 |date=22 May 2003}} scoring on his comeback game against [[Warrington Wolves]]. In 2004, he spent time playing for [[Hunslet Hawks]] in [[National League Two]].{{cite web |title=Hold-up in Thunder bid |url=https://www.chroniclelive.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/hold-up-in-thunder-bid-1628710 |website=Evening Chronicle |access-date=25 March 2025 |date=28 August 2004}} He played once more for Castleford Tigers in 2005 against Hull Dockers in the Challenge Cup.{{cite web|title=Tigers' roar heard after early strike by Dockers|url=http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/news/features/tigers-roar-heard-after-early-strike-by-dockers-1-2556790|website=Yorkshire Post|access-date=30 July 2014|date=13 March 2005 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140808062323/http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/news/features/tigers-roar-heard-after-early-strike-by-dockers-1-2556790 |archive-date=8 August 2014}} Overall Sampson played 431 games for Castleford scoring 68 tries. With 431-appearances, Sampson is joint second (along with [[Arthur Atkinson (rugby league)|Artie Atkinson]]) in Castleford's all-time appearance list behind [[John Joyner]], who has 613-appearances. Sampson was a real fans favourite for Castleford and his name was often chanted by the home fans. ===International honours=== Sampson played at {{rlp|pr}} in [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]]'s 14–8 victory over [[Auckland Rugby League|Auckland]] on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australasia]] at [[Carlaw Park]], [[Parnell, New Zealand]] on Wednesday 8 July 1992, and played at {{rlp|pr}} in [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]]'s 17–6 victory over [[Canterbury Rugby League|Canterbury]] on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australasia]] at [[Rugby League Park]]/[[Rugby League Park|Addington Showgrounds]], [[Christchurch]] on Wednesday 15 July 1992. In the 1997 post season, Sampson was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in the first match of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=76|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084550/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=76|archive-date=16 February 2012 }} Sampson won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Castleford in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]] against Fiji, South Africa ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and Wales ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and in 1999 against France (two occasions). ==Coaching career== After retirement, Sampson became the club's academy coach and won the Junior academy championship in 2004. He left the club in May 2005,{{cite web |title=Sampson cuts ties with Castleford |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/4566583.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=25 March 2025 |date=20 May 2005}} and moved to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and became the assistant coach there. Sampson left Hull Kingston Rovers after a brief period and stayed out of the game for a while. Sampson rejoined Castleford for the [[2009 Super League season]], and was the club's academy coach once again.{{cite web |title=Tigers sign new academy coaches |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/7684826.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=25 March 2025 |date=22 October 2008}} ==Honoured at Castleford Tigers== Sampson is a [[Castleford Tigers|Tigers]] Hall of Fame Inductee.{{cite web|url=http://castigers.com/cgi-bin/halloffame.cgi|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060506212435/http://www.castigers.com/cgi-bin/halloffame.cgi|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 May 2006|title=Hall of Fame at castigers.com|publisher=castigers|date=31 December 2008|access-date=1 January 2009 }} ==Genealogical information== Sampson is the son of the rugby league footballer, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]; [[David Sampson (rugby league)|Dave Sampson]] and Mavis ([[née]] Dean), the nephew of the rugby league footballer; [[Malcolm Sampson]], and the cousin of the [[Sprint (running)|sprinter]]; [[Denise Ramsden (athlete)|Denise Ramsden]], and the rugby union, and rugby league footballer; [[Paul Sampson]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==External links== *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20070929104732/http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm World Cup 1995 details] *{{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120518154425/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=76|date=18 May 2012|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk }} *{{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120518154425/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/image_uploads/matches/170/000502sampson.jpg|date=18 May 2012|title=Playing picture at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk }} *{{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120518154425/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/pictures/profiles/sampsond.jpg|date=18 May 2012|title=Coach picture at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk }} *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20100114185422/http://totalrl.com/features/content.php?feat_id=268&featcat_id=32 England battle to French wins] {{England 1995 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{Great Britain squad – 1992 Tour}} {{1999 Super League Dream Team}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sampson, Dean}} [[Category:1967 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:England national rugby league team players]] [[Category:English rugby league coaches]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Gold Coast Chargers players]] [[Category:Great Britain national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Parramatta Eels players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wakefield]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:20th-century English sportsmen]] [[Category:Hunslet R.L.F.C. players]]
